title: AlarmRules
description: Deterministic, explicit alarm rules. The alarm engine evaluates these against live tags — no ML, no inference. Each rule maps one tag + condition to a severity, with debounce/deadband to stop chatter. Aligns with ISA-18.2 lifecycle concepts.

tag.description: Tag to evaluate; must exist in tag_map.json.

asset_id.description: Owning asset (denormalized for fast status derivation); must exist.

severity.description: Drives node color + Calm Card severity + priority.

priority.description: 1 highest. Used for ordering when not using time-to-consequence.

condition.description: The trigger. Supports a single threshold or warning/critical bands.

critical.description: Band form: critical level. Must be more severe than warning (validator checks logical ordering).

for_ms.description: Debounce: condition must hold this long before the alarm raises.

deadband.description: Hysteresis: value must recover past threshold±deadband before clearing. Prevents flapping. Cannot be negative.

delay_ms.description: Additional raise delay. Cannot be negative.

latching.description: If true, stays active until explicitly acked even after value recovers.

requires_ack.description: Critical alarms should require ack (validator warns if a critical rule has requires_ack=false).

shelvable.description: Operator may shelve (OPC-UA shelving). Shelving is audited with reason/owner/expiry.

max_shelve_seconds.description: Required if shelvable; suppression without expiry is rejected.

suggested_actions.description: Hints; the authoritative actions live in action_envelope.
